budget_wines.name: E. Guigal Cotes du Rhone rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely distributed in France at major supermarkets and wine merchants (Nicolas, Monoprix); also available at Guigal's Ampuis cellar door and export markets worldwide.
budget_wines.description: Guigal's entry-level Cotes du Rhone rouge is one of the most reliable under-EUR15 reds in France. Grenache-led with Syrah for spice and Mourvedre for structure, it offers the classic southern Rhone profile at a supermarket price point.
budget_wines.tip: The Guigal CDR rouge holds for up to three years from vintage. Buy a case from the Ampuis cellar door and compare it with the much pricier Cote-Rotie to understand the Guigal house style at scale.

budget_wines.name: E. Guigal Cotes du Rhone blanc
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Nicolas and major supermarkets across France; export markets via standard Guigal distribution. Cellar door at Ampuis.
budget_wines.description: Guigal's Cotes du Rhone blanc is Viognier-dominant, giving it apricot-blossom and stone-fruit aromatics at a fraction of the cost of Condrieu. Drink young for the freshest Viognier expression.
budget_wines.tip: At under EUR15 this is one of the best-value Viognier-driven white wines in France. Serve chilled with a Provencal fish dish.

budget_wines.name: Perrin & Fils La Reserve Cotes du Rhone rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Very widely distributed in France (Carrefour, Leclerc, Monoprix, Nicolas) and exported globally; cellar door at the Perrin Families' Chateau de Beaucastel estate.
budget_wines.description: The Beaucastel family's entry-level Cotes du Rhone rouge, made under the Perrin et Fils label. Grenache-dominant with savoury herbes de Provence character and approachable tannins. The best-value introduction to the Beaucastel house philosophy.
budget_wines.tip: The Perrin Reserve rouge is also the gateway to understanding Grenache-Syrah-Mourvedre blending. Compare it with Beaucastel proper to trace the terroir and selection effect.

budget_wines.name: La Vieille Ferme rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: One of France's most widely distributed wines; available in virtually every French supermarket and in export markets across Europe, North America, and Asia.
budget_wines.description: La Vieille Ferme is the Perrin family's Luberon-region brand, producing reliable Grenache-dominant reds and whites at under EUR10. The rouge is consistently one of France's best-selling wines at its price point, offering genuine Rhone Valley character in an entry-level format.
budget_wines.tip: La Vieille Ferme is the house wine of thousands of French bistros for good reason. The blanc (Grenache Blanc, Bourboulenc, Ugni Blanc) is also worth finding.

budget_wines.name: Delas Freres Saint-Esprit Crozes-Hermitage rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at Nicolas and speciality wine shops in France; widely exported. Cellar door at Maison Delas in Saint-Jean-de-Muzols, Ardeche.
budget_wines.description: Delas Freres is one of the oldest Rhone negociant houses (founded 1835) and their Saint-Esprit Crozes-Hermitage is the most accessible entry into the northern Rhone style: all-Syrah, with black-pepper, smoked-meat and violet aromatics at a price that beats most comparable Syrah worldwide.
budget_wines.tip: The Saint-Esprit can be drunk within two years of harvest or cellared for five to seven years. It offers an honest Crozes typicity without the oak-weight of the prestige cuvees.

budget_wines.name: Domaine le Clos des Cazeaux Vacqueyras rouge Cuvee des Templiers
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at the Vacqueyras cave cooperative and the domaine cellar door in Vacqueyras village; also distributed through specialist wine merchants in France and the UK.
budget_wines.description: Vacqueyras is often overlooked in favour of neighbouring Gigondas but offers comparably structured Grenache-Syrah blends at a lower entry price. The Clos des Cazeaux Templiers combines old-vine Grenache with Syrah for a structured, garrigue-scented red that drinks well at four to ten years.
budget_wines.tip: Vacqueyras sits just north of Chateauneuf-du-Pape and shares the galets roulants soil types in some parcels. The Clos des Cazeaux is one of the most consistent producers in the appellation.

budget_wines.name: Domaine de la Mordoree Tavel La Dame Rousse rose
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Available at the Mordoree cellar door in Tavel and through specialist wine merchants in France and the UK. Widely distributed in northern European export markets.
budget_wines.description: Tavel is France's most celebrated dry rose appellation and Domaine de la Mordoree is one of its benchmark producers. La Dame Rousse is the entry-level Tavel: copper-salmon in colour, full-bodied, with flavours of ripe strawberry, white peach, and Mediterranean herbs. Unlike Provence roses, Tavel can age three to five years.
budget_wines.tip: La Dame Rousse is an ideal all-day food wine in the south of France: substantial enough for lamb cutlets, light enough for fish and aioli. Drink at cellar temperature, not ice-cold.

budget_wines.name: Chateau d'Aqueria Lirac rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Chateau d'Aqueria cellar door near Tavel; Nicolas and independent wine merchants in France; exported to UK and Benelux.
budget_wines.description: Lirac AOC, on the right bank of the Rhone opposite Chateauneuf-du-Pape, produces structured Grenache-Syrah blends at consistently lower prices than its famous neighbour. Chateau d'Aqueria's Lirac rouge combines garrigue character with dark-fruit concentration and is one of the appellation's most reliable examples at under EUR20.
budget_wines.tip: Lirac is one of the Rhone's best-value appellations. The d'Aqueria white (Grenache Blanc, Clairette) is also worth trying at a similar price.

budget_wines.name: Yves Cuilleron Saint-Joseph Les Vignes d'a Cote rouge
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Yves Cuilleron cellar door in Chavanay; speciality wine merchants in France, UK, Belgium, and United States via Kermit Lynch importer.
budget_wines.description: Yves Cuilleron's Les Vignes d'a Cote is the entry-level Saint-Joseph from one of the northern Rhone's most respected producers. All-Syrah from younger vines, it shows the violet, black-pepper, and smoked-olive character of the appellation with less oak weight than the prestige cuvees. Accessible from release, it gives a fair picture of Saint-Joseph's character at under EUR25.
budget_wines.tip: Cuilleron makes a parallel Condrieu (Terreur de Vergisson) and a full range of Syrah cuvees; a cellar-door visit in Chavanay lets you taste the full ladder from Les Vignes d'a Cote up through the prestige La Petite Cote Condrieu.

budget_wines.name: Cave de Rasteau Rasteau rouge Ortas Tradition
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Cave de Rasteau co-operative cellar door in Rasteau village; widely distributed through French wine merchants and exported to northern Europe.
budget_wines.description: The Rasteau cooperative's Ortas Tradition rouge is an accessible entry into the Rasteau AOC, which received full communal appellation status in 2010. Grenache-dominant with Syrah and Mourvedre, it shows the southern Rhone's characteristic ripe-fruit and garrigue warmth at a co-operative price.
budget_wines.tip: The cave co-operative also offers a direct introduction to Rasteau VDN (fortified) styles alongside the dry red. The tasting room in the village is open year-round.
